other_observers: Seen with Tony Leukering, the Allertons, Joe Himmel and Richard Maxfield.; First seen by Susan Allerton, and seen by many.
duration: Seen six times mostly for between one and two minutes
specific_location: Residence of Susan and Peter Allerton, which is about six miles northeast of Durango.
description: A hummingbird similar in size to the Black-chinned and Broad-taileds that were also present. The most obvious field mark was the black mask and white eye stripe. The mask went from the base of the bill, below the eye and down onto the cheek. The bold eye stripe was right above this from the eye down to the neck. Both the mask and the eye stripe were wide, bold and the dominant face features. The bill was of medium length, straight and vaguely red, especially over the inner half. The top of the head, back of the neck, back and central tail were all a darkish green. The breast was considerably lighter, with green spots especially on the upper breast. The wings and outer tail looked very dark or black. The legs and feet also looked dark.Behaviors: Mostly seen coming into three hummingbird feeders at a residence, but also seen feeding at a nearby blackberry bush.Call: None noted.Plumage: Adult
